title: Since when does my GF force ME to watch NGE
display_title: Since when does my GF force ME to watch NGE
wikitext:
[]: Since when does my GF force ME to watch NGE?             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Mon Feb 20, 2012 9:11 pm Back in the olden days, I used to pressure her to watch it with me almost every day, but as time went on, I lost an interest and now my GF wants me to regain it for some reason. Somehow, though, I've been trying to avoid it until tonight - she's made me download the series in its entirety with the intent of watching it with me (we watched Inuyasha, Full Metal Alchemist, One Piece, and Monster together in the same fashion). Am I embarrassed of my former obsession with Evangelion? Do I fear her disappointment, or, at worst, her insane laughter? I don't know what she'll think! Do I take it like a man and manage to regain what was lost to me? Or do I run like fuck and hope I never have to worry about it? "I mustn't run away" seems more fitting now than ever...

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Mon Feb 20, 2012 9:37 pm You gotta be a man for yo girl, guy.

[]:             Quote Post by r0ach » Mon Feb 20, 2012 10:04 pm When I tried getting my girl to watch Eva she laughed and hated it so much. Typically in my experience the two don't go together very well. You should live it up

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Mon Feb 20, 2012 10:10 pm I know a two girls who love Evangelion long before I ever heard about it. If she's been through that many series with you, I doubt there will be anything different about Evangelion for her. Until you get to the tonal shift midway through the series... that will be the real test.

[]:             Quote Post by Kamon-san » Mon Feb 20, 2012 10:53 pm You know, I wouldn't be too worried about the change of tone halfway through the series... for all we know, this is what could get her REALLY interested in the show. Didn't the original Full Metal Alchemist anime go through a change of tone towards the end of the series too?

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Mon Feb 20, 2012 11:58 pm Kamon-san wrote: You know, I wouldn't be too worried about the change of tone halfway through the series... for all we know, this is what could get her REALLY interested in the show. Didn't the original Full Metal Alchemist anime go through a change of tone towards the end of the series too?

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Tue Feb 21, 2012 12:05 am Evangelion is a unique test of mental fortitude and, though I haven't personally experienced this, I suspect that would go double for a relationship. Tell us how it goes!

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Tue Feb 21, 2012 4:03 am Take it from Shinji: Be a pussy, get th.... well, you know how it goes.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka » Tue Feb 21, 2012 5:00 am I used to force my bf to watch it with me, but he gave up after 6 or 8 episodes...cause he is a pussy ;) I'm going to get the whole series (including EoE and D&R) and watch it with him in one sitting - no excuses then!

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Tue Feb 21, 2012 9:17 am Fireand'chutes77 wrote: Take it from Shinji: Be a pussy, get th.... well, you know how it goes.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Tue Feb 21, 2012 9:50 am >Your girlfriend will never tie you up and make you watch animu against your will.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Tue Feb 21, 2012 10:59 am Your gf is making you watch Eva with her? You're a lucky guy if you ask me. Tell us how she reacts to the latter half and EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Tue Feb 21, 2012 12:01 pm Asuka wrote: I used to force my bf to watch it with me, but he gave up after 6 or 8 episodes...cause he is a pussy ;)

[]:             Quote Post by r0ach » Tue Feb 21, 2012 3:37 pm driftking18594 wrote: A user named Asuka has a boyfriend who is a pussy. Sounds familiar...

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Tue Feb 21, 2012 5:38 pm ONIAgent150 wrote: I know a two girls who love Evangelion long before I ever heard about it. If she's been through that many series with you, I doubt there will be anything different about Evangelion for her. Until you get to the tonal shift midway through the series... that will be the real test.

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Tue Feb 21, 2012 7:21 pm How long has it been since she first saw it and what drew her back to it?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Feb 21, 2012 8:37 pm I actually hated Evangelion when I first watched it...but it kept sucking me back in and by Episode 16 I was riveted.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Tue Feb 21, 2012 9:04 pm ONIAgent150 wrote: How long has it been since she first saw it and what drew her back to it?

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Tue Feb 21, 2012 10:10 pm Ah, so it sounds like she is getting interested as a direct result of you not being interested? Is this something that's happened in the past? Like you stop loving Egg McMuffins and she starts liking them? Or is this solely an NGE thing?

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Wed Feb 22, 2012 7:05 pm ONIAgent150 wrote: Ah, so it sounds like she is getting interested as a direct result of you not being interested? Is this something that's happened in the past? Like you stop loving Egg McMuffins and she starts liking them? Or is this solely an NGE thing?
